data.accept_first_seen_users: Accept first event from unknown users

data_description.activity_filter: Default filter used when a user has no custom activity override.
data_description.update_profile: Conservative keeps the current slow polling behavior. Balanced keeps the same fetch strategy with a more responsive intent. Adaptive fast uses Garmin postTrackPointFrequency to avoid requesting new points before Garmin is likely to have published them.
data_description.user_agent: HTTP User-Agent sent to Garmin. Leave the default unless Garmin behavior or troubleshooting requires a custom value.
data_description.expose_debug_attributes: Expose advanced troubleshooting attributes such as API/page status and poll task state on session entities.

abort.single_instance_allowed: Only one Garmin LiveTrack configuration is allowed

error.invalid_user_agent: User-Agent must be 256 characters or fewer
error.invalid_allowed_activities: Select at least one activity when using custom activity mode

init.title: Garmin LiveTrack
init.description: Update global Garmin LiveTrack settings. Optionally choose one user to edit.

data.accept_first_seen_users: Accept first event from unknown users

data_description.allowed_users: Comma-separated Garmin display names. Free text is kept so you can pre-register a user before their first LiveTrack arrives.
data_description.activity_filter: Global default. A user custom activity selection overrides this value.
data_description.update_profile: Conservative is safest for Garmin and long activities. Balanced aims for better dashboard responsiveness. Adaptive fast still respects Garmin publishing cadence and only requests trackpoints when a new point is likely to be available.
data_description.user_agent: HTTP User-Agent sent to Garmin for page and API requests.
data_description.expose_debug_attributes: When enabled, session entities expose advanced troubleshooting attributes such as API/page status and poll task state.
data_description.edit_user: Select a configured user to edit per-user policy.

user_policy.title: User Policy
user_policy.description: Update Garmin LiveTrack policy for {user}. Allowed activities are only used when activity mode is set to custom.

data_description.mode: normal tracks normally, register_only records the user but does not track, one_event_only allows one accepted event before disabling.
data_description.user_activity_mode: inherit_global uses the global default activity filter. custom overrides it for this user.
data_description.allowed_activities: Only used when Activity filter mode is custom.

error.invalid_user_agent: User-Agent must be 256 characters or fewer
error.invalid_allowed_activities: Select at least one activity when using custom activity mode

garmin_shape_change_suspected.title: Garmin LiveTrack response shape may have changed
garmin_shape_change_suspected.description: Garmin LiveTrack returned repeated missing session or missing trackpoint responses. The integration has seen {count} consecutive anomaly signal(s), which often means Garmin changed the public site or API response shape. Check diagnostics, verify the configured HTTP User-Agent, and compare with a working LiveTrack in a browser before assuming the activity itself is gone.
